rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical dehydrogenative regioselective cyclization of 3-aza-1,5-dienes with 1,3-dicarbonyl compounds to construct 4-pyrrolin-2-ones via anodic generation of 1,3-dicarbonyl radicals.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the 1,3-dicarbonyl compound (Eox p/2 = 1.49 V vs Ag/AgCl) to a 1,3-dicarbonyl radical; a second anodic SET oxidizes the cyclized C-radical to a cation; concomitant cathodic reduction of protons evolves H2.

key_intermediates.name: open-chain radical adduct (B) from radical addition onto the acryloyl C=C of the 3-aza-1,5-diene
cyclized C-radical (C) after intramolecular radical trapping by the enaminic double bond
cationic intermediate (D) from SET oxidation of C at the anode

electron_flow: The anode oxidizes the 1,3-dicarbonyl compound to a C-centered radical (A), which adds regioselectively to the acryloyl alkene of the 3-aza-1,5-diene to give radical B; intramolecular cyclization onto the enaminic C=C gives radical C, which is further oxidized at the anode to cation D, and deprotonation delivers the pyrrolinone while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Yield of 3a suppressed to <1%, indicating a radical intermediate.

probe_reagents.name: p-dinitrobenzene (pDNB)
probe_reagents.observation: SET scavenger; 0% yield, supporting single-electron transfer.

probe_reagents.name: current on/off experiment
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction halts during off cycles, excluding a radical chain mechanism.

probe_reagents.name: H2 headspace detection
probe_reagents.observation: H2 concentration increases over time, confirming cathodic proton reduction paired with anodic oxidation.

notes: Regioselectivity favors radical addition onto the acryloyl alkene rather than the aryl-substituted enaminic alkene, with cyclization closing through the enaminic double bond rather than via aryl radical attack. On/off current experiments rule out a radical chain propagation; instead a stepwise anodic SET / radical addition / cyclization / second anodic SET / deprotonation sequence is invoked, paired with cathodic H2 evolution.
